hero.name: Richard J. Valeo, CSM
hero.title: Implementation Manager · AI-Powered Customer Engagement Platforms
hero.value: 15+ years leading technical onboarding, requirements discovery, and platform configuration for SaaS and AI-powered solutions — with direct experience integrating CRMs, APIs, and cloud platforms for distributed, multi-country client organizations. I own the implementation lifecycle from first discovery call to measurable time-to-value.

the_case.requirement: 4+ years hands-on technical role; 1+ years delivering customer implementations
the_case.evidence: 15+ years of client-facing technical delivery: Understood.org (CRM integration + replatform implementation), Eptura (SaaS platform adoption across ~10 teams), Crocs (technical discovery + workflow configuration), OVRSTCH (founder-led platform build)
Azure/Kubernetes at Eptura, Crocs, Understood.org; API integrations at OVRSTCH (Stripe, WooPay, ApplePay, Printify); Salesforce CRM integration at Understood.org

the_case.requirement: Project management across multiple simultaneous client implementations
the_case.evidence: Understood.org: managed a distributed implementation team across 12+ countries; Eptura: coordinated implementation and adoption across ~10 concurrent teams

the_case.requirement: Translating complex technical concepts to non-technical stakeholders
the_case.evidence: Primary technical point of contact for VP of Engineering (Understood.org) and Operations Board (Eptura); consistently the bridge between engineering and business stakeholders
Founder of OVRSTCH (built and configured an entire AI-native commerce platform solo); engagement lead at a stealth-mode AI startup navigating ambiguous, fast-moving requirements
Direct hands-on Salesforce CRM integration work at Understood.org (2019–2020), delivered alongside a full platform replatform

the_case.requirement: Familiarity with intent-based and generative AI
the_case.evidence: Daily practitioner of Claude Code, ChatGPT/GPT-4, and Google Gemini since 2022; led delivery for a startup building fine-tuned LLM models on these same platforms against proprietary datasets

the_stack.description: Implementation and integration tooling used daily or with direct delivery experience

when_things_break.scenario: A client implementation stalls because requirements were never fully understood before configuration began
when_things_break.response: At Understood.org, I inherited a distressed, multi-year program and led technical discovery to fully understand the CRM integration and replatform requirements before proceeding — delivering with zero critical regressions after taking over in its final crucial months.

when_things_break.scenario: Multiple concurrent client/team implementations start competing for the same limited technical resources
when_things_break.response: At Eptura, I coordinated SaaS platform adoption and configuration across ~10 distributed teams spanning two continents, standardizing cadences and building executive dashboards so leadership could see where resourcing conflicts were emerging before they became blockers.

when_things_break.scenario: A solo implementation needs to integrate multiple third-party APIs with no dedicated engineering team
when_things_break.response: At OVRSTCH, I personally configured and integrated payment APIs (Stripe, WooPay, ApplePay) and automated fulfillment (Printify API) as the platform's founder, then diagnosed and resolved a production bottleneck that was costing 25% of fulfillment cycle time.

how_i_run_a_room: Every implementation starts with structured technical discovery — understanding the client's actual requirements before any configuration begins
Client-facing status updates translate technical progress into business terms before the client has to ask
RAID log maintained and reviewed at every client touchpoint; integration risks named before they become blockers
Configuration decisions are documented as they're made, not reconstructed after the fact
Retrospectives after each implementation feed directly into faster time-to-value on the next one
Upsell/cross-sell opportunities get flagged as they surface during discovery, not left for account management to find later

sr_pm_partner.before: Client implementations stall in discovery, technical requirements get lost in translation to the account team, and time-to-value slips past what was promised in the sales cycle
sr_pm_partner.after: Clear discovery-to-configuration process, technical decisions translated into business terms as they happen, and a client relationship that survives the handoff from sales to delivery

the_ramp.day_one: Review the current client implementation pipeline, shadow an active onboarding call, and read existing configuration documentation for the core Level AI products — Auto-QA, Analytics, Voice of the Customer, Agent Assist, Screen Recording
the_ramp.thirty_days: Own end-to-end implementation for at least one active client account; identify 2-3 process improvements in the current onboarding workflow; establish working relationships with sales, product, and engineering counterparts
